results.title: KVTuner: Sensitivity-Aware Layer-Wise Mixed-Precision KV Cache Quantization for Efficient and Nearly Lossless LLM Inference
results.snippet: Layer-wise sensitivity-aware KV cache quantization across mixed precision; layers with higher importance receive more bits — a budget-redistribution result conceptually similar to redistributing freed budget to surviving units.

results.title: OBCache: Optimal Brain KV Cache Pruning for Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference
results.snippet: Cache eviction as layer-wise structured pruning problem; quantifies token saliency by attention-output perturbation. Drops low-saliency tokens, lets surviving tokens carry more weight — functional analog of trading density for size.

results.title: DepthKV: Layer-Dependent KV Cache Pruning for Long-Context LLM Inference
results.snippet: Allocates KV budgets across layers according to relative importance under a fixed global memory constraint. The constraint surface is explicitly a fixed budget; layers traverse the constraint surface as input length grows — direct functional analog of the Reineke frontier.

results.title: The Sparse Frontier: Sparse Attention Trade-offs in Transformer LLMs
results.snippet: Explicitly frames the Pareto frontier of sparse attention; an 'efficiency crossover' where sparsification enables larger sparse models to outperform smaller dense ones. The frontier IS a log-log relationship on accuracy vs sparsity.

results.title: Expected Attention: KV Cache Compression by Estimating Attention
results.snippet: Compression via expected attention reallocates budget across the cache; freed slots are not deleted but recycled to high-attention positions.

raw_responses.query: attention head pruning rank trade-off transformer dynamic head count rank growth survivor heads 2025

results.title: Making Every Head Count: Sparse Attention Without the Speed-Performance Trade-off
results.snippet: Explicitly addresses the head-pruning-vs-performance trade-off; surviving heads receive concentrated load. The trade-off curve is the constraint frontier.

results.title: Analyzing Multi-Head Self-Attention: Specialized Heads Do the Heavy Lifting, the Rest Can Be Pruned
results.snippet: Foundational head-pruning result: ~80% of heads can be pruned with minimal accuracy loss. Surviving heads specialize as positional / syntactic / rare-word. Direct functional analog of forest self-thinning where surviving stems specialize as canopy / understory.

results.title: Efficient self-attention with smart pruning for sustainable large language models
results.snippet: 2025 Nature Scientific Reports paper on attention pruning; 'smart' pruning preserves surviving heads' representational capacity along a fixed efficiency-accuracy frontier.

results.snippet: Allometric scaling under self-thinning. Source-domain reference, tangential to candidate's LLM bridge.
